title: Best Microchip Cat Doors of 2026

picks.label: BEST OVERALL
picks.name: SureFlap - Sure Petcare Microchip Cat Flap, White, Scans Pet's ID on Entry, Check Your Cat's Size, Flap Opening is 4 3/4" (H) by 5 5/8" (W)
keySpecs: Flap opening 5 5/8" W x 4 3/4" H with 2.75" tunnel depth
Stores up to 32 cat microchip identities
Programming retained in memory even with batteries removed
4-way manual lock (in-only, out-only, locked, open)
AA-battery powered with flashing low-battery indicator
evidence: PetsRadar: testers describe the SureFlap Microchip Cat Flap as 'reliable, robust and simple,' with one-button chip registration and a flashing low-battery indicator
PetsRadar: stores 32 cat identities and keeps its programming in memory even when batteries are removed; runner-up in their 5-flap roundup behind only the pricier Connect bundle
PetsRadar: flap opening 5.59" x 4.72" with 2.75" tunnel depth; 4-way lock system

TheCatSite (forum thread 'Best chip door'): one long-term owner reported exterior-mounted SureFlap mechanisms accumulate dirt and grime that can eventually jam the locking gear — interior installs last longer
consHints: Manual 4-way lock only — no app, timer, or curfew features
Requires a separate mounting adapter for glass or metal door installation
One TheCatSite owner reported the smaller SureFlap cat door gave 'all sorts of problems from minor annoyances to total breakdown' on a weather-exposed exterior install
Flap opening is on the small side for large breeds — Sure Petcare itself says to check your cat's size

picks.label: BEST VALUE
picks.name: PetSafe Microchip Cat Door, Exterior or Interior Pet Door - Multi-User RFID Access Up to 40 Pets, 4-Way Locking, Weatherproof, DIY Easy Install, Hardware Kit; Privacy for Cat Litter Box or Pet Feeder
keySpecs: Registers up to 40 pets — the highest chip memory of any pick in this guide
Flap opening 5.5" x 5.9"
4-way locking with two magnetic closing points to reduce drafts
One-button microchip programming
Low-battery warning flashes every 10 seconds; works for exterior doors or interior litter-box/feeder rooms
evidence: PetsRadar: 'Best for simplicity' pick — one-button programming, two magnetic closing points reduce drafts, energy-efficient design
PetsRadar: registers up to 40 cats, a higher chip memory than SureFlap's 32 or Cat Mate Elite's 9
PetsRadar: low-battery warning flashes every 10 seconds so you don't get surprise lockouts

PetSafe Support: glass installation requires a professional glazier cutting a circular hole, and tempered or double-pane glass cannot be cut after manufacture — program and test the door BEFORE cutting
consHints: Manual lock system only — no app, curfew, or exit control
Requires a separate adapter for metal doors, walls, or double-glazed glass
PetsRadar notes it is not compatible with all microchip types — verify your cat's chip format before buying
TheCatSite owners report determined cats destroying lighter plastic-frame doors by pushing and forcing

picks.label: BEST FOR MULTI-CAT HOUSEHOLDS
picks.name: SureFlap DualScan Microchip Cat Door - Door Opening is 4 3/4" H x 5 5/8" W
keySpecs: DualScan technology scans on entry AND exit — set per-cat exit permissions
Keeps indoor-only cats inside while outdoor cats come and go through the same door
Safety mode lets an indoor-only cat re-enter if it escapes through an open door or window
Stores up to 32 pet identities, retained without batteries
Up to 12 months on 4 AA batteries with red low-battery flash; flap opening 5 5/8" W x 4 3/4" H
evidence: Sure Petcare (official): 'Set exit permissions for each cat to keep specific pets indoors using DualScan technology' — individual entry and exit permissions per registered cat
Sure Petcare (official): Safety mode means 'indoor-only pets can gain entry if they escape through an open door or window'
Sure Petcare (official): 32-pet memory held without batteries; up to 12 months battery life on 4 AA with a red low-battery indicator
Sure Petcare (official): manual lock plus in-only mode that lets a cat come indoors without being able to exit again
consHints: No app or connectivity — exit permissions are set on the device, not remotely
Same compact flap opening as the classic SureFlap; tight for cats over ~13 lbs
$200 for a non-connected door is a hard sell unless you specifically need the indoor-only exit control
TheCatSite consensus: any door mixing electronics and moving mechanical parts will wear out — expect under ~10 years even from the best brands

picks.label: PREMIUM SMART UPGRADE
picks.name: SureFlap Microchip Pet Door Connect Without Hub - Flap Opening is 6 3/4 inches by 7 inches
keySpecs: Largest flap in this guide: 6 3/4" x 7" opening fits big cats and small dogs
App curfew mode and remote lock/unlock via the Sure Petcare app (requires Hub, sold separately)
Entry/exit notifications and long-term activity monitoring per pet
Registers up to 32 pets; up to 10 people can share app access
Roughly six months on 4 AA batteries; 3-year warranty
evidence: SmartHomeExplorer: leads the smart pet-door category on ID Accuracy (9.5) and Stray Exclusion (9.5) — Sure Petcare's wide-read-zone RFID antenna geometry reduces false lockouts when a pet approaches off-axis

PetsRadar (Connect hands-on review): curfew settings and remote lock/unlock via the Sure Petcare app; entry/exit notifications helped the reviewer track down a missing cat; verdict 'a worthy investment'
PetsRadar (Connect hands-on review): registers up to 32 cats with up to 10 app users; about six months of battery life on four AA batteries; 3-year warranty
PetsRadar (Connect hands-on review): one tester's cat learned to pick the lock mechanism — SureFlap replaced the unit free with an improved design

TheCatSite (forum thread 'Best chip door'): a long-term owner is on her second large SureFlap pet door — the first survived three years of daily exterior use by three cats before the locking mechanism failed, and shrugged off a 14-lb cat trying to force it
consHints: This ASIN ships WITHOUT the Hub — app features require the separately-sold Sure Petcare Hub plus a live internet connection, and go dark during outages
At $307.65 street it is the most expensive door in the guide, before adding the Hub
The 6 3/4" x 7" opening needs a much larger cut than cat-flap-sized rivals — less reversible for renters or resale

passedOn.name: SureFlap Microchip Cat Door Connect with Hub Bundle (B07FGS5YYJ)
passedOn.reason: PetsRadar's best-overall pick and the editorially-preferred Connect+Hub SKU, but the Amazon listing resolved with no live buy-box price (price: null on repeated Creators API checks 2026-06-10) — we don't recommend a SKU readers can't actually buy. The Pet Door Connect (B071PDLN8L) carries the same Connect platform with a live price.

passedOn.name: Cat Mate Elite Microchip Cat Flap with Digital Timer & LCD Display Bundle (B08TCJR5W8, $112.98)
passedOn.reason: Verified live, but it only stores 9 cat identities vs 32-40 for the picks, PetsRadar flags a fiddly battery installation, and the live US ASIN bundles a wall liner most door installs don't need. PetSafe wins the budget slot on capacity and simplicity.

Sure Petcare official product pages (DualScan, Pet Door Connect specs)

TheCatSite forum (multi-year owner durability thread 'Best chip door')
SmartHomeExplorer.com (sister-network smart pet door consensus guide — RFID read-zone and stray-exclusion scoring)
communitySources: TheCatSite 'Best chip door' thread (primary community evidence this session)
r/CatAdvice (recurring microchip-door recommendation threads; not directly indexed in this session's searches)
faqSeeds: Do microchip cat doors work with every microchip? (some budget flaps are incompatible with 10-digit lettered chips — verify your cat's chip format)
Can you install a microchip cat door in a glass door or window? (yes via adapter, but a professional glazier must cut the hole, and tempered or double-pane glass can't be cut after manufacture)
How long do batteries last in a microchip cat door? (typically 4 AA batteries for 6-12 months, with a flashing low-battery indicator)
Will a microchip cat door keep raccoons and stray cats out? (yes — the flap stays locked unless a registered chip is read)
Can I let one cat outside while keeping another cat indoors? (only with dual-scan exit control like the SureFlap DualScan — standard microchip doors only scan on entry)
Do I need the Hub for the SureFlap Connect to work? (no — it works as a standard microchip door without it, but app control, curfews, and notifications require the separately-sold Hub and internet)
comparisonAxes: Flap opening dimensions (cat-only 5 5/8" x 4 3/4" vs large 6 3/4" x 7")
Exit control (entry-only scan vs DualScan per-cat exit permissions)
Smart features (app curfew/notifications vs manual 4-way lock; Hub requirement)
Battery life and low-battery warning (6 vs 12 months on 4 AA)
Installation surface support (wood door vs glass/metal/wall adapter requirements)
